translations.translation:
Liu Yan (Junlang)
劉焉 (君郎)
Lived ADca.140–194
aSanguozhiBiographyRecords of the Three Kingdoms [三國志]
Scroll 31 (Shu 1)
byZL181
onMarch 15th, 2025

Liu Yan, styledJunlang, was fromJingling[竟陵],Jiangxia[江夏].
A distant descendant of theRespectfulPrinceofLu[魯恭王] underHan1shifted fiefdom toJingling[竟陵] during theera of theGentleEmperor, their side branch then residingthere.2•••
PrinceLiu Yu[劉餘] was the son of the sixth emperor of theHan,Liu Qi.↩
Here, “side branch” [支庶] refers to the descendants of a son who was not the primary heir and was not born of the main wife.↩
When[Liu] Yanwas young, he served his province and commandery. As he was an imperial clansman, he was conferred asHousehold Gentleman, but later left office when his teacher,ExcellencyZhu [祝公], died.AHe resided at Yangcheng mountain [陽城山], accumulating knowledge and giving instruction. He was nominated asWise and GoodandFoursquare and Forthright, recruited into the bureau of theExcellency over the Masses, then served asPrefectofLuoyang[洛陽令],InspectorofJi province[冀州刺史],Grand AdministratorofNanyang[南陽太守],Minister of the Imperial Clan, andMinister of Ceremonies.
Your minister,[Pei] Songzhi, would inspect this.ExcellencyZhu [祝公] was theExcellency over the Masses, Zhu Tian [祝恬].
[Liu] Yanobserved that the governance of theKeenEmperorwas declining and the royal clan was with many problems, so he introduced a proposal, saying, “TheGrand AdministratorsandInspectorsbribe to serve in office and harm the commoners, thus inducing strife and revolt. We ought to nominate important officials of pure reputations to serve as governors so that they may protect and secure lands across the realm.” But[Liu] Yanreally requested to be theGovernorofJiaozhi[交阯牧] because he wanted to evade the difficulties of the world. The discussion had not been carried out whenPalace AttendantDong FuofGuanghan[廣漢] privately told[Liu] Yan, “The capital will soon be in chaos, and the zone3ofYi province[益州] has the aura of theSon of Heaven.” After[Liu] Yanheard what[Dong] Fusaid, he then wanted to be inYi province[益州].•••
Here, “zone” [分野] is the astrological belief that certain lands on earth corresponded with certain zones in the heavens. Anything that happened in the zones of heavens, such as comets, supernovas, and other astronomical occurrences, was said to give clues to the future of the lands on earth. For example,Yi provincewas associated with the Turtle Beak [觜] and Triad [参] Mansions, which were basically constellations.↩
It happened that theInspectorofYi province[益州刺史],Xi Jian, levied taxes in a vexing manner, with rumors of such spreading afar,BthenBing province[并州] killed theirInspector, Zhang Yi [張壹], andLiang province[涼州] killed theirInspector, Geng Bi [耿鄙]. This would fulfill the plot of[Liu] Yan, for he then exited to serve asAssigned Army Supervisor,GovernorofYi province[益州牧], and given fief asMarquisofYangcheng[陽城侯] so that he could arrest[Xi] Jianand punish him for his crimes.C[Dong] Fualso requested to serve asChief CommandantofShu Dependent States[蜀郡屬國都尉], and theDirector of the Imperial Granary,Zhao WeiofBaxi[巴西], left his office, so they both accompaniedhim.D
[Xi] Jianwas the grandfather of[Xi] Zheng.
TheBook of Continued Hanstates: At the time,Liu Yuwas appointed to serve inYou province[幽州],Liu Yanto serve inYi province[益州],Liu Biaoto serve inJing province[荊州], andJia Congto serve inJi province[冀州].[Liu] Yuand the others were all gentlemen with clean reputationswithin the Seas, and some were selected to become governors from being aMinisterorSecretariat Minister. Each of them retained their original salaries from the [original] ranks they held. In accordance with the old rites, they were transported by carriage drawn by three horses and with side curtains that were red.
Your minister,[Pei] Songzhi, would inspect this. After theKeenEmperordied, righteous troops rose up andSun Jiankilled theInspectorofJing province[荊州刺史],Wang Rui. It was afterwards thatLiu Biaoserved inJing province[荊州], not at the same time as[Liu] Yan.
TheBiographies of the Elders in Yi ProvincebyChen Shoustates:Dong Fuwas styledMao’an. When he was young, he followed a teacher to study, and he comprehended multiple classics. He enjoyed the Ouyang commentary of theBook of Documentsand he also served the hermit scholar Yang Hou [楊厚], thoroughly learning charts and prophecies. When he arrived at the capital, he travelled to read at the Imperial Academy, then returned home to teach, his disciples coming from afar. In the first year of theera [167], when there was a solar eclipse, an edict the nomination of scholars who would beWise and GoodandFoursquare and Forthrightand the advantages and disadvantages of policy questions.4Zhao Qianand others ofZuopingyi[左馮翊]5recommended[Dong] Fu, but[Dong] Fudid not go due to illness. He petitioned a sealed memorial afar toChang’an[長安], claiming severe illness and that he would return home. The government [tried to] recruit him ten times in total, and the chariot of anExcellencywas used to summon him thrice, and he was again nominated asWise and GoodandFoursquare and Forthright,Academician, andOne with Morality, but he would not assume them all. His name and reputation was especially good. TheGrand General,He Jin, petitioned to nominate[Dong] Fu,stating:•••
Here, “policy questions” [策問] were given to examinees who were to respond to political questions and scenarios, and if they got a rank, it supposed to be based on the quality of their answers.↩
The edict in the previous sentence would have been directed towards local officials for them to give examinations and recommend officials, hence the vague wording here likely implies that Zhao Jian is a high-ranking official ofZuopingyi, and that he was not necessarily fromZuopingyi.↩
“He can provide the virtue ofYouandXia,6and he can relate the way ofMister Kong. Within him, he holds the technique of elimination and rejuvenation by Jiao [焦] and Dong [董] dear to him.7Right now,Bing[并] andLiang[涼] are in turmoil and the western Rong barbarians are in rebellion. We should order for the chariot of anExcellencyto be used to specially summon him and treat him with extraordinary respect so that we may consult him for any geniusplans.”•••
Yan Yan[言偃], styledZiyou[子游], andBu Shang[卜商], styledZixia[子夏], were both disciples ofKong Qiu, helping spread his teachings after he died.↩
Jiao Gan [焦贛] and Dong Zhongshu [董仲舒] were notable Ruists who helped spread Ruism during theHan dynastywith their works on Ruist classics. They presumably espoused the notion of eliminating anything abnormal and returning to a state of normalcy.↩
Hence, theKeenEmperorsummoned[Dong] Fuand bestowed him to bePalace Attendant. Within the court, he was called “The Ruist”. He requested to serve asChief CommandantofShu Dependent States[蜀郡屬國都尉]. It had been one year after[Dong] Fudeparted when theKeenEmperordied and the world fell into great bedlam. Later, he left his office and died at the age of eighty-two at his household.
When[Dong] Fufirst spoke and argued, he had little competition inYi province[益部], hence he was called the “Harbinger of Cessation”. There were no speakers who could equal him such that whenever he arrived, conversations would stop. Later,Assisting ChancellorZhuge LiangaskedQin Miwhat[Dong] Fuexcelled at, and[Qin] Misaid, “Dong Fupraised virtue, even if they were as fine feathers, and criticized wickedness, even if they were as minutegaps.”8•••
Here, “fine feathers” [秋毫] and “minute gaps” [纖芥] are both metaphors for extremely minute things, “fine feathers” being so tiny on a bird and “minute gaps” being so tiny one may not notice it.Qin Miis saying thatDong Fuwould praise the smallest of virtuous deeds and he would criticize the smallest of wicked deeds.↩
At the time, the rebelling traitors ofYi province[益州],9Ma XiangandZhao Zhi, among others, declared themselves to beYellow ScarvesatMianzhu county[綿竹縣], gathering people exhausted from labor, and within one to two days, they obtained thousands of people. They first killed thePrefectofMianzhu[綿竹令],Li Sheng, and the officials and common people flocked together, merging with them to form over ten thousand people. They then advanced to attackLuo county[雒縣], then attackedYi province[益州] and killed[Xi] Jian, then advanced toShu commandery[蜀郡] andJianwei[犍為], and within a month, they ravaged the three commanderies.[Ma] Xiangdeclared himself theSon of Heaven, his host in the tens of thousands. The provincialClerk,Jia Long, led hundreds of troops to be at the eastern side ofJianwei[犍為] and gathered officials and common people, obtaining over one thousand people. He then attacked[Ma] Xiangand the others, and after several days, they were defeated and routed, and the province was pacified.[Jia] Longthus selected officials to quickly welcome[Liu] Yan.•••
Some Chinese texts erroneously write this asLiang province, notYi province.↩
[Liu] Yanmoved the government seat toMianzhu[綿竹], appeased and recruited those who broke away and revolted, and conducted his deeds with lenience and kindness, but he secretly plotted to have treacherous designs. The mother ofZhang Lubegan to use Ghost Daoism10and had a young appearance. She often went out and in the household of[Liu] Yan.[Liu] Yanthus sent[Zhang] Luto serve asMajor who Supervises Justiceand station atHanzhong[漢中] to block off the plank bridges of the valleys and kill messengers ofHan.[Liu] Yanthen petitioned, “The Rice Brigands have cut off the roads, hence we cannot longer communicate any further.” He also used some other pretext to kill over ten major personages of the province, among themWang XianandLi Quan, and instituted strict criminal law.ETheGrand AdministratorofJianwei[犍為太守],Ren Qi, andJia Longthus rebelled and attacked[Liu] Yan, but[Liu] Yanstruck and killed[Ren] Qiand[Jia] Long.F•••
This is another name for the Five Pecks of Rice Daoist sect.↩
TheMiscellaneous Records of the Elders in Yi Provincestates:Li Quanwas styledBoyu. He served asChiefofLinqiong[臨邛長]. His son was[Li] Fu, who can be seen in theAppraisal of Contributing MinistersbyYang XiofJianwei[犍為].
TheRecords of Famed Heroesstates: WhenLiu Yanraised his troops, he did not suppressDong Zhuoalongside the world, but protected his province and secured himself. TheGrand AdministratorofJianwei[犍為太守],Ren Qi, declared himself to be General and joined withClerkChen Chaoto muster troops to attack[Liu] Yan, but[Liu] Yanattacked and defeated them.Dong Zhuosent theExcellency over the Masses,Zhao Qian, to lead troops to the province and persuadeColonelJia Longto send his troops back to attack[Liu] Yan.[Liu] Yansent out the Azure Qiang to fight with them, and he was thus able to defeat and kill [them].[Ren] Qiand[Jia] Longwere all fromShu commandery[蜀郡].
The desires of[Liu] Yanswelled even more, and he manufactured imperial chariots and vehicles, having over one thousand vehicles. TheGovernorofJing province[荊州牧],Liu Biao, petitioned, arguing that[Liu] Yanwas resemblingZixiabeing suspected of being theSagewhile inXihe[西河].11At the time, of the sons of[Liu] Yan,[Liu] Fanserved asLeft Household General,[Liu] Danserved asImperial Clerk Preparer of Documents, and[Liu] Zhangserved asCommandant who Receives the Chariot, and they all followed theAstuteEmperorto reside inChang’an[長安].GOnly his son,12Major of a Separate Unit[Liu] Mao, followed[Liu] Yan. TheAstuteEmperorsent[Liu] Zhangto exhort[Liu] Yan, but[Liu] Yankept[Liu] Zhangand did not send him back.HAt the time, theGeneral who Conquers the West,Ma Teng, garrisoned atMei[郿] and rebelled.[Liu] Yan, alongside[Liu] Fan, plotted with[Ma] Tengto lead troops to strikeChang’an[長安]. The plot by[Liu] Fanwas leaked and he fled toHuaili[槐里], but[Ma] Tengwas defeated and returned toLiang province[涼州].[Liu] Fanwas killed at that time and[Liu] Danwas arrested and executed.IGentleman ConsultantPang XiofHenan[河南] had interconnected familial ties with[Liu] Yan, so he sought and led the grandsons of[Liu] Yaninto Shu [蜀].•••
Bu Shangtaught widely in theXiheregion and caused the people to think that he was on the same level asKong Qiu.Liu Biaois implying thatLiu Yanis trying to become the emperor.↩
It is debated if “son” here should mean “little son” [小子] or “third son” [叔子]. “Little son” is used in some older texts, butLiu Maowas actually the third son ofLiu Yan, so “third son” should be more appropriate.↩
TheRecords of Famed Heroesstates: The father of[Liu] Fan,[Liu] Yan, served asGovernorofYi province[益州牧]. Of the requisitionsDong Zhuosent to him, none arrived. Thus,[Liu] Fanand his brothers, three people in total, were locked up atMei castle[郿塢], being restrained by being placed in a dark cell.
TheSummarized Documentsstates: At the time,[Liu] Zhangserved asCommandant who Receives the Chariotand was in the capital.[Liu] Yanclaimed illness as a pretext to summon[Liu] Zhang, and[Liu] Zhangpetitioned that he needed to check on[Liu] Yan. Thereafter,[Liu] Yankept[Liu] Zhangand did not return him.
TheRecords of Famed Heroesstates:[Liu] Fanfled to the camp ofMa TengfromChang’an[長安] and requested troops from[Liu] Yan.[Liu] YansentColonelSun Zhaoto lead troops to reinforce them, but they were defeated atChang’an[長安].
At the time,[Liu] Yansuffered from natural fires burning the cities and destroying all his chariots, even extending to the households of the common people.[Liu] Yanrelocated his government seat toChengdu[成都]. He was sorrowful over his sons and he was also spooked by the ominous disasters. In the first year of theera [194], a carbuncle broke out on his back and he died. Major provincial officials, among themZhao Wei, desired for the gentleness and kindness of[Liu] Zhang, so they petitioned that[Liu] Zhangserve asInspectorofYi province[益州刺史]. An edict appointed him to serve asAssigned Army SupervisorandGovernorofYi province[益州牧].[Zhao] Weiwas appointed asHousehold General who Conquers the Eastand he led troops to attackLiu Biao.J
TheRecords of Famed Heroesstates: When[Liu] Yandied, his son,[Liu] Zhang, succeeded him asInspector. It happened thatChang’an[長安] appointedHu MaoofYingchuan[潁川] to serve asInspector, and he enteredHanzhong[漢中]. TheAssistant OfficialofJing province[荊州別駕],Liu He, and the rebelling generals of[Liu] Zhang,Shen Mi,Lou Fa, andGan Ning, attacked[Liu] Zhangbut did not triumph, so they fled intoJing province[荊州], garrisoning atQurun[朐䏰].
The upper character [Qu [朐]] is pronounced Chun [蠢] and the lower character [Run [䏰]] has the fanqie of Ru [如] and Zhen [振].
